speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Now old desire doth in his deathbed lie,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And young affection gapes to be his heir.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: That fair for which love groaned for and would die,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: With tender Juliet matched, is now not fair.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Now Romeo is beloved and loves again,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Alike bewitchèd by the charm of looks,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: But to his foe supposed he must complain,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And she steal love’s sweet bait from fearful hooks.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Being held a foe, he may not have access

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: To breathe such vows as lovers use to swear,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And she as much in love, her means much less

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: To meet her new belovèd anywhere.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: But passion lends them power, time means, to meet,
